mappings.evidence_note: Title page states 'English Language Curriculum and Assessment Guide (Secondary 4 - 6)', jointly prepared by CDC and HKEAA, published by Education Bureau HKSARG, 2021, effective from Secondary 4 in the 2021/22 school year.
Aims of the English Language Curriculum: increase language proficiency for study/work/leisure/personal enrichment; develop knowledge, skills, values and attitudes; promote lifelong learning.
Curriculum framework comprises subject knowledge and skills, generic skills, positive values and attitudes; organised under Interpersonal, Knowledge and Experience Strands.

Interpersonal Strand targets: establish/maintain relationships and routines; converse/discuss/compare/argue/evaluate/justify; communicate complex messages; participate in planning/organising events; obtain/provide objects/services/information.

Knowledge Strand targets: provide/find/select/analyse/organise/present information; interpret/use complex information; identify/discuss ideas/issues/themes/arguments/views; identify/define/solve problems; develop/reorganise ideas; understand how English works.

Experience Strand targets: respond to imaginative/literary texts; respond to characters/events/issues/themes; give expression to imaginative ideas and personal experience through oral/written/performative means.
Language Forms and Communicative Functions: grammatical forms/structures, vocabulary, text types; detailed list in Appendix 1 (p.79-83).
Language Skills and Language Development Strategies for Key Stage 4 (S4-6): listening, speaking, reading, writing and development strategies; detailed in Appendix 4 (p.91-94).
Attitudes Specific to English Language Learning; detailed in Appendix 5 (p.95-96).
Curriculum Organisation: Learning Time, Modules of Learning (Compulsory Part + Elective Parts), Enrichment Components.

Reading assessment objectives: understand/interpret purpose and meaning; identify main theme/key details; contextual meaning; tone/mood; distinguish/evaluate views/arguments; language features; interpret/analyse/select/organise ideas from sources.

Writing assessment objectives: write for different contexts/audiences/purposes; convey meaning with vocabulary/devices/patterns; plan/produce coherent structured texts; appropriate tone/style/register/genre; draft and revise.
Listening assessment objectives: understand/interpret spoken texts; key details; speakers' feelings/views/attitudes/intentions; range of accents/varieties; language features in spoken texts.

Speaking assessment objectives: express information/ideas with elaboration; convey meaning with vocabulary/patterns; establish/maintain exchanges; produce coherent structured speeches; pronunciation; pace/volume/intonation/stress/eye contact/gesture.
Paper 1 Reading: weighting 20%, duration 1.5 hours.
Paper 2 Writing: weighting 25%, duration 2 hours.
Paper 3 Listening and Integrated Skills: weighting 30%, duration about 2 hours.
Paper 4 Speaking: weighting 10%, duration about 20 minutes.
School-based Assessment: weighting 15%, reading/viewing programme, two to four texts over three years (at least one print and one non-print), assessed through oral performance.
Public assessment encompasses reading, writing, listening and speaking; includes SBA component; graded approach in reading and listening/integrated skills papers.
Standards-referenced reporting: five performance levels (1-5), Level 5** and 5* annotations; SBA moderated statistically.
Appendix 1: Language Items and Communicative Functions for Key Stages 3-4.
Appendix 3: Examples of Text Types for Key Stages 1-4.
Appendix 4: Language Skills and Language Development Strategies for Key Stage 4 (S4-6).

draft_notes: Page numbers use printed/footer numbers. Roman numeral p.i for preamble; Arabic numerals from p.1 for main body.
Physical PDF page offset is approximately +2 for main body (printed p.1 = physical page 3).
